title: Add MaxParams6L_120 submission (1.2374 BPB) to track_non_record_16mb
body:
Add 6L SwiGLU submission (1.2374 BPB, 1xH100) 

  Summary                                                         

  - 6-layer encoder-decoder (3+3 with learned skip connections), all other submissions use 9-11 layers  
  - SwiGLU MLP (1280 hidden) instead of universal ReLU-squared
  - Untied embeddings with embed_lr=0.6 (12x higher than competition)                                    
  - Full MHA (8 KV heads) instead of GQA                                                                 
  - Per-dimension learned controls: resid_mix, attn_scale, mlp_scale, q_gain (~18K params in fp32)       
  - INT8 + zlib, 13.5MB artifact, 19.15M parameters                                                      
                                                                                                         
  Result                                                                                                 
                                                                                                         
  - 1.2374 BPB on 1x H100 in 10 minutes (12.5% of the 8xH100 compute budget)                             
  - Within 0.013 BPB of the 8xH100 baseline (1.2244)
  - Only 31% of training schedule completed (6,245/20,000 steps) — loss still decreasing                 
                                                                                                         
  Key Insight                                                                                            
                                                                                                         
  Under fixed wallclock, fewer layers = faster steps = more learning. 6 layers complete 4,222 steps vs   
  3,338 for 9 layers (+26%), and the extra optimization outweighs the lost depth.
